reviewer: Codex independent bilingual audit (separate agent; not the candidate author)

methodology.scope: Every candidate row was inspected for Czech and English quality, bilingual equivalence, target annotation/usefulness, age 6-10 safety, difficulty/CEFR, grammar/clause/topic/scene metadata, and exact/semantic duplication against canonical and within the batch.
methodology.levelPolicy: Level 1 was held to the stricter ‘very very simple’ requirement; Level 2 remained the main volume and had to be short, concrete, and inferable.
methodology.semanticDuplicateRule: A row fails when it preserves the same practical meaning and target context as a canonical row while adding only filler such as today, now, here, possession, intensity, or word order. Distinct modality, safety context, referent specificity, or evaluative meaning may remain as an explicitly noted acceptable near-neighbor.

promotionRecommendation.cleanSubset: The 219 passing rows may be promoted only after the batch is split, project-owned corpus licensing is explicitly confirmed, review metadata is updated, and the compiled corpus is revalidated.
promotionRecommendation.failedSubset: Revise or reject all 31 failed rows. Corrected rows require a fresh independent bilingual and duplicate review before promotion.
reasons: Nineteen candidate rows are insufficiently distinct semantic repeats of canonical learner sentences.
The remaining failed rows contain Czech/English naturalness or equivalence problems, grammar/topic metadata errors, or insufficiently explicit technology context.
The candidate provenance currently says licensing confirmation is required; that separate release blocker must be resolved before any promotion.
strengths: All 250 rows are valid UTF-8, fully NFC, structurally valid, child-safe, and have exact target surface/token annotations.
The intended distribution is appropriate: 62 Level 1 rows and 188 Level 2 rows, with no undeclared Level 3 material.
No exact Czech or English duplicate exists against canonical or within the candidate batch.
Most rows are short, natural, concrete, and materially improve weak-target branching coverage.

findings.message: The English reverses the learner-facing construction: ‘Bojím se tmy’ directly means ‘I am afraid of the dark’, while ‘The dark scares me’ maps the target verb to a different English predicate.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘I am afraid of the dark.’ and update the scene query.

findings.message: This is the canonical ‘Bolí mě břicho. / My stomach hurts.’ with only ‘teď / now’ added; it does not provide a meaningfully different branch.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a genuinely different, natural context for ‘břicho’, or reject the row.

findings.message: This is the canonical ‘Bolí mě hlava. / My head hurts.’ with only ‘dnes / today’ added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a genuinely different, child-safe context for ‘hlava’, or reject the row.

rows.english: My little brother is building a tower.

rows.czech: Pan Novák má dnes hodně práce.
rows.english: Mr. Novák has a lot of work today.
Tomáš is happy about the trip.
Ema is nervous before the race.

rows.czech: Petr je na svůj obrázek pyšný.
rows.english: Petr is proud of his picture.
We will visit family at the weekend.
Our neighbor has a friendly dog.
The teacher writes a word on the board.
My friends are waiting outside school.
I am bringing a book to my friend.
Anička is reading a short story.
Dad is coming home at six.
The baby is sleeping peacefully in the crib.
Grandpa is cooking soup for lunch.

findings.message: This closely repeats ‘Podlaha je mokrá. / The floor is wet.’; ‘tady / here’ is only a deictic filler and does not add a distinct learning context.
findings.suggestedAction: Author a substantially different sentence for ‘podlaha’, or reject the row.

rows.english: The keys are lying beside the door.
Put the paper in the bin.
I feed our cat in the morning.
We will wash the dishes after dinner.
The book belongs on the top shelf.
Pick up the blocks from the rug.
Move the plant closer to the window.
Turn on the lamp by the bed.
Water the small flower on the table.
Sweep the crumbs under the table.
Please take out the full bin.

findings.message: This closely repeats ‘Pokoj je uklizený.’ and adds only ‘teď / now’.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a different use of ‘uklizený’, or reject the row.

rows.english: We will clean the kitchen together.
Wipe up the water by the bath.
The toys are in the blue box.
The clock is one minute fast.

findings.message: ‘Tato polévka je teplá.’ is semantically the same as canonical ‘Polévka je teplá.’; the demonstrative does not create a useful new branch.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a distinct soup context, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Naše snídaně je hotová.’ differs from canonical ‘Snídaně je hotová.’ only by possession.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a distinct breakfast action or context, or reject the row.

rows.english: Mom is chopping an onion for the soup.

findings.message: This repeats canonical ‘Chléb je čerstvý. / The bread is fresh.’ with only ‘dnes / today’ added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a materially different bread context, or reject the row.

rows.english: We will put the apples in a bowl.
Put the spoon beside the plate.
The jug is full of water.

findings.message: This is close to ‘Pojďme se podělit o sušenku.’, but the modal ‘můžeme / can’ teaches a genuinely different function, so it remains acceptable.

rows.english: David spilled milk on the table.
Please do not eat so fast.

findings.message: ‘Nejez prosím tak rychle.’ is effectively the same instruction as canonical ‘Nejez moc rychle.’.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a different natural context for ‘rychle’, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Omyj jablko pod vodou’ literally places the apple under/submerged in water and is not the natural hygiene instruction.
findings.suggestedAction: Use a natural phrase such as ‘Omyj jablko pod tekoucí vodou.’ and re-evaluate the target and difficulty.

findings.message: ‘Wash the apple under water’ is unnatural English for this instruction.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘Wash the apple under running water.’ and update the scene query.

rows.english: Add a little honey to the tea.

findings.message: This repeats canonical ‘Napiš své jméno. / Write your name.’ with only ‘sem / here’ added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a distinct use of ‘jméno’, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Sleduj tabuli’ is not the ordinary classroom instruction; Czech normally says ‘Dívej se na tabuli’ or refers to what is shown on the board.
findings.suggestedAction: Rewrite as a natural classroom instruction while preserving an exact playable target.

findings.message: The declared skill ‘imperative location’ does not describe ‘tabuli’, which is an accusative direct object in the authored sentence.
findings.suggestedAction: Correct the skill focus and grammar tag after rewriting.

findings.message: ‘Mám jednu otázku.’ is semantically the same as canonical ‘Mám otázku.’; ‘jednu / one’ adds no useful branch distinction.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a different question context, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Přečti tuto větu.’ repeats canonical ‘Přečti větu.’ with only a demonstrative added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a genuinely different sentence-reading context, or reject the row.

rows.english: Work with a partner for a while.
Say the answer in a full sentence.
I do not know the right answer yet.
Can you explain this example to me?
The lesson ended exactly at ten.
Check the last line in the notebook.
I sit next to my friend.
We will ask the teacher for help.
Find one mistake in the text.
Catch the blue ball with both hands.
Kick the ball between two cones.
Roll the die one more time.
Draw a house beside the tree.
Build a bridge with four blocks.
Color the roof with a red pencil.

findings.message: This repeats canonical ‘Ta hra je zábavná. / The game is fun.’ with only ‘opravdu / really’ added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a distinct context for ‘zábavná’, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Teď je řada na tobě.’ has the same meaning as canonical ‘Teď jsi na řadě. / It is your turn.’.
findings.suggestedAction: Use a different natural context for ‘tobě’, or reject the row.

rows.english: Let us take turns after each move.

findings.message: ‘Po jednom tahu’ means one move at a time, not after each move; the English changes the turn-taking rule.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘Let us take turns, one move at a time.’ or revise both languages together.

findings.message: This is close to ‘Najdi chybějící dílek’, but ‘skládačky / puzzle’ adds a concrete, useful referent and is acceptable.

rows.english: Dance to the rhythm of this song.
Fold the paper as shown in the picture.
Hang the picture above the table.
Let us choose a new game.
We will make a team of three players.
We have two bags in the car.
I sit by the window on the bus.
The journey to school is long.
A small plane is flying over the city.
Please take a seat by the window.

findings.message: ‘Přisednout si’ normally means joining/sitting next to someone; ‘Přisedni si k oknu’ is awkward for the intended instruction to take a window seat.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘Sedni si prosím k oknu.’ or another context that naturally licenses ‘přisednout si’.

rows.english: Cross the street at the crossing.
Fasten your seatbelt before the ride.
The bus will stop around the corner.
The station is busy in the morning.

findings.message: This is close to ‘Na nádraží je rušno’, but ‘ráno’ adds a useful routine-time context and is acceptable.

rows.english: We are walking along a quiet street.
We wait outside at the stop.

findings.message: ‘We wait outside at the stop’ is stilted and does not naturally render the ongoing Czech situation.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘We are waiting outside at the bus stop.’ and update the scene query.

rows.english: We are looking for a free seat on the train.
I need to get off at the next stop.
The train has a short delay.

findings.message: ‘Vlak má malé zpoždění.’ repeats canonical ‘Vlak má zpoždění.’; the adjective does not create a meaningfully different branch.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a different context for ‘zpoždění’, or reject the row.

rows.english: Wait for the green light before crossing.

findings.message: This extends ‘Počkej na zelenou’ with the concrete crossing context, which is a useful safety distinction and is acceptable.

rows.english: Do not forget your travel card.

findings.message: ‘Nezapomeň si cestovní kartu’ omits the verb required by the reflexive construction and is not a clean standard sentence.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘Nezapomeň si vzít cestovní kartu.’ or ‘Nezapomeň na cestovní kartu.’, then re-tokenize and re-evaluate difficulty.

rows.english: The ball is behind the box.
The pharmacy is beside the library.
The café is on the corner.
The keys are on the table.
The bus stop is over there.
I will go there with you.
Our classroom is on the right.
The toilet is at the end of the hall.
The backpack is under the chair.
I will show you a shorter route.
The tower stands by the river.
We can meet by the school.
The library is opposite the school.
The bike is in front of the house.
The boy got lost on his way home.
Please step away from the door.

findings.message: This repeats canonical ‘Obloha je modrá. / The sky is blue.’ with only ‘dnes / today’ added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a substantially different sky context, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Vítr fouká.’ is only a word-order variant of canonical ‘Fouká vítr.’ and has the same meaning.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a different wind context, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Dnes prší.’ repeats canonical ‘Prší. / It is raining.’ with only ‘today’ added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a different rain context, or reject the row.

findings.message: ‘Vezmi si teplou bundu’ means take a warm jacket (for example, take it with you), not put it on.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘Take a warm jacket.’ or change Czech to ‘Obleč si teplou bundu.’

findings.message: This is close to ‘Nasaď si čepici’, but the blue attribute supplies a concrete selection task and is acceptable.

rows.english: The shoes are wet after the rain.
It will be sunny in the afternoon.
It is snowing quietly outside the window.
I need a warm coat today.
The white shirt hangs in the wardrobe.
Anna is putting on a new dress.

findings.message: The skill/tag ‘imperative clothing’ is incorrect: ručníky are towels, and the sentence expresses bringing/direction, not clothing.
findings.suggestedAction: Retag this as an imperative object/direction pattern.

rows.english: We have bad weather for the trip.
It will be cloudy in the morning.
We have enough time before lunch.
The library closes at five o'clock.
I will come in ten minutes.
We will start one more game.

findings.message: ‘Hurry to the bus’ is not the natural English equivalent of ‘Pospěš si na autobus’, which means hurrying in order to catch it.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘Hurry to catch the bus.’ and update the scene query.

rows.english: I will come home before dinner.
The bus will arrive in five minutes.
We are going on a trip next week.
The film ended ten minutes ago.
Five minutes remain until the break.
Please slow down at the bend.

findings.message: ‘Zpomal prosím u zatáčky’ is a transport/road-safety instruction, not a time-and-routine record; its topic, objective, and topic grammar tag are inconsistent with the sentence.
findings.suggestedAction: Move it to transport/road safety and update objective and topic tag.

rows.english: I get up at seven every morning.
I need to get up early tomorrow.
I eat breakfast with my family at weekends.
I usually eat dinner around six.
Charge the phone before the trip.

findings.message: This is close to ‘Obrazovka je jasná’, but ‘příliš / too’ changes a neutral property into a usability problem and is acceptable.
This is close to ‘Stiskni tlačítko’, but the green attribute creates a useful selection instruction and is acceptable.

rows.english: Save the picture in the folder.

findings.message: This repeats canonical ‘Baterie je skoro vybitá.’ with only possession added.
findings.suggestedAction: Replace with a materially different battery context, or reject the row.

findings.message: English normally describes a battery as almost dead, nearly flat, or low—not ‘almost empty’.
findings.suggestedAction: Use a natural battery expression if the row is rewritten.

rows.english: Please turn on the desk lamp.
Photograph the map by the entrance.

findings.message: Without an explicit computer/app context, ‘zavři všechna okna / close all the windows’ is equally a household instruction. The declared technology topic and generic scene query are not inferable from the visible sentence.
findings.suggestedAction: Make the software context explicit and update the scene query while preserving the target ‘Nejdřív’.

rows.english: Do not share your password with anyone.

findings.message: The verb ‘sdílet’ takes the thing shared and normally ‘s + instrumental’ for the person; ‘Nikomu nesdílej’ uses the wrong valency.
findings.suggestedAction: Use ‘S nikým nesdílej své heslo.’ or another natural safety instruction.

findings.message: The intended meaning duplicates canonical ‘Nesdílej své heslo. / Don’t share your password.’.
findings.suggestedAction: Prefer a different password-safety context rather than only adding ‘with anyone’.

rows.english: I cannot hear you through the headphones.
The old computer is a little slow.
